{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Supervisor",
  "description": "Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview


	We are seeking an experienced and hands-on Logistics Supervisor to lead distribution and warehouse operations within a fast-paced tyre and automotive products business.
	The role is responsible for ensuring efficient inbound and outbound logistics, optimal inventory control, and timely delivery to customers, dealers, and retail partners.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	HND/BSc. in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3&ndash;4 years of experience in logistics or supply chain operations, preferably within:
	Proven experience managing warehouse and delivery operations in Lagos.
	Familiarity with ERP or inventory management systems is an added advantage.


Salary
N200,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;cv@ascentech.com.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

Logistics Supervisor Job at Ascentech Services Limited




        
    

        
        Ascentech Services Limited is a full-fledged Human Resource Solution Provider incorporated in 2013 with its Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to forging long-term partnerships with our clients by creating and delivering robust and flexible services that address the changing needs of their business.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Supervisor

Job Location: Isolo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Position Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking an experienced and hands-on Logistics Supervisor to lead distribution and warehouse operations within a fast-paced tyre and automotive products business.
	The role is responsible for ensuring efficient inbound and outbound logistics, optimal inventory control, and timely delivery to customers, dealers, and retail partners.


Qualifications & Experience


	HND/BSc. in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Minimum of 3–4 years of experience in logistics or supply chain operations, preferably within:
	Proven experience managing warehouse and delivery operations in Lagos.
	Familiarity with ERP or inventory management systems is an added advantage.


Salary
N200,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Importation and Bank Reconciliation Officer (Desk & ERP)",
  "description": "Nigeria Sinotruck Limited is a commercial automotive company that is interested in starting a vendor leasing desk.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Importation and Bank Reconciliation Officer (Desk &amp; ERP)
Job Location:&nbsp;Surulere (Head Office), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to: Supply Chain &amp; Logistics Manager (Dotted Line to Treasury Officer)

Role Purpose


	As the Importation and bank reconciliation officer (Desk &amp; ERP), your role will be pivotal in prompt and correct bookkeeping on each batch of importation from PFI to Insurance, to Form M, to LC, to bank payment, clearing activities to delivery of imported goods to our premises.
	Your focus will lie in ensuring documentation and booking on ERP all transactions on each batch of importation.
	Prompt booking, weekly and monthly reconciliation of each batch of importation, international partners GL update and schedule of balances, also, Monthly submission of bank reconciliation for Naira and domiciliary accounts.


Description of Contribution


	Prompt documentation and booking of new batch of importation and daily update of activities (Insurance, Form M, LC payments etc).
	Prompt update of international partners GL and provision of schedule to support balances.
	Responsible for GLs of each GIT and schedule to support balances.
	Raise Purchase request on local truck and semitrailer purchase, prompt update on Vendor GL and schedules.
	Ensure daily posting of all bank transactions (local and domiciliary accounts).
	Submission of monthly bank reconciliation of all banks (local and domiciliary accounts).
	Others as may be assigned by CFO.


Entry Requirements


	Minimum Qualification(s): HND or B.Sc
	Years of Experience: 1 - 2 years of experience in accounts department of similar company.


Type of Experience (Hand on experience):


	Proficiency in utilizing accounting software and Microsoft tools.
	Excellent bookkeeping and bank reconciliation skills.
	Possesses excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Capable of working both independently and collaboratively within a team
	Demonstrated experience in leadership roles.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;fafolabi13@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-04-16",

Importation and Bank Reconciliation Officer (Desk & ERP) Job at Nigeria Sinotruck Limited




        
    

        
        Nigeria Sinotruck Limited is a commercial automotive company that is interested in starting a vendor leasing desk.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Importation and Bank Reconciliation Officer (Desk & ERP)
Job Location: Surulere (Head Office), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports to: Supply Chain & Logistics Manager (Dotted Line to Treasury Officer)

Role Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	As the Importation and bank reconciliation officer (Desk & ERP), your role will be pivotal in prompt and correct bookkeeping on each batch of importation from PFI to Insurance, to Form M, to LC, to bank payment, clearing activities to delivery of imported goods to our premises.
	Your focus will lie in ensuring documentation and booking on ERP all transactions on each batch of importation.
	Prompt booking, weekly and monthly reconciliation of each batch of importation, international partners GL update and schedule of balances, also, Monthly submission of bank reconciliation for Naira and domiciliary accounts.


Description of Contribution


	Prompt documentation and booking of new batch of importation and daily update of activities (Insurance, Form M, LC payments etc).
	Prompt update of international partners GL and provision of schedule to support balances.
	Responsible for GLs of each GIT and schedule to support balances.
	Raise Purchase request on local truck and semitrailer purchase, prompt update on Vendor GL and schedules.
	Ensure daily posting of all bank transactions (local and domiciliary accounts).
	Submission of monthly bank reconciliation of all banks (local and domiciliary accounts).
	Others as may be assigned by CFO.


Entry Requirements


	Minimum Qualification(s): HND or B.Sc
	Years of Experience: 1 - 2 years of experience in accounts department of similar company.


Type of Experience (Hand on experience):


	Proficiency in utilizing accounting software and Microsoft tools.
	Excellent bookkeeping and bank reconciliation skills.
	Possesses excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
	Capable of working both independently and collaboratively within a team
	Demonstrated experience in leadership roles.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

COVID-19 forced an unprecedented experiment in flexible working, which is now driving a surge in demand for our flexible workspace solutions. To meet this demand, we are rapidly accelerating the growth of our global network. To deliver our ambition we are recruiting partners to open and operate IWG locations. We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Customer Service Executive

Job ID: 50433
Job Location:&nbsp;Jos, Plateau
Employment Type: Full Time (On-site)

	This position sits at the heart of our growing global organisation and is a critical role where you will have an opportunity to shine and deliver world-class customer service.
	You will be part of a dynamic team with amazing career opportunities, working regular and sociable hours and with the chance to unleash your full potential.


We will expect you to:


	Give our customers and their guests a warm and friendly welcome, every day.
	Manage a range of on-site tasks to help our busy workspace run smoothly and happily.
	Above all, help your customers and colleagues have a great day at work.


About you

	Great communication skills and a love of interacting with people.

	The motivation to be your best every day &ndash; and the determination to be even better tomorrow.


What we can do for you
You are reading the right advert if you are looking for:


	A fun, challenging and rewarding career.
	Award-winning induction training and excellent ongoing learning and development.
	Fantastic promotion prospects.
	Generous, achievable incentives and sociable hours.
	Access to a comprehensive range of flexible, personalised workplace benefits that support mental, physical and financial wellbeing.

Customer Service Executive Job at International Workplace Group (IWG)




        
    

        
        International Workplace Group (IWG) is the largest provider of flexible workspace solutions in the world. Trading under a number of brands (including Regus, Spaces, HQ, Signature and No18) we have a physical network of over 3,000 locations across 1,000 towns and cities in 120 countries. Our mission is to help our millions of customers to have a great day at work, every day.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



COVID-19 forced an unprecedented experiment in flexible working, which is now driving a surge in demand for our flexible workspace solutions. To meet this demand, we are rapidly accelerating the growth of our global network. To deliver our ambition we are recruiting partners to open and operate IWG locations. Our target audience are building owners, franchise operators and competitors, we will work with our partners under a variety of commercial structures demanding a dynamic and broad-based approach to business development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive

Job ID: 50433
Job Location: Jos, Plateau
Employment Type: Full Time (On-site)

Description


	This position sits at the heart of our growing global organisation and is a critical role where you will have an opportunity to shine and deliver world-class customer service.
	You will be part of a dynamic team with amazing career opportunities, working regular and sociable hours and with the chance to unleash your full potential.


We will expect you to:


	Give our customers and their guests a warm and friendly welcome, every day.
	Manage a range of on-site tasks to help our busy workspace run smoothly and happily.
	Above all, help your customers and colleagues have a great day at work.


About you
What really matters is that you’ve got the right mindset. We’ll provide everything else for you to build a fantastic career with IWG. All you need to bring is:


	Great communication skills and a love of interacting with people.
	A positive, outgoing and can-do attitude.
	The ability to take direction, learn new skills and apply them with confidence.
	The motivation to be your best every day – and the determination to be even better tomorrow.


What we can do for you
You are reading the right advert if you are looking for:


	A fun, challenging and rewarding career.
	Award-winning induction training and excellent ongoing learning and development.
	Fantastic promotion prospects.
	Generous, achievable incentives and sociable hours.
	Access to a comprehensive range of flexible, personalised workplace benefits that support mental, physical and financial wellbeing.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Stock Auditor",
  "description": "AAVA Brands Limited, a new generation company of Chanrai Summit Group (Fareast Mercantile Co. Limited), has been proudly serving Nigeria since 1890 and connects world-class brands to the Nigerian consumer, every day. Now under the umbrella of DP World, AAVA Brands Limited is further strengthened as a flexible and agile service provider. Keeping pace with the evolving needs of African consumers, we strive harder to make the right products available at the right place, in the right time.

We have dedicated our resources to supply chain management through the import / manufacture and distribution of products covering branded Food, Beverages and Fast Moving Consumer Goods for the best multi-nationals in the world. A product portfolio spanning 3,500 SKUs is managed by capable, professional teams, robust infrastructure, and strong delivery systems to ensure availability to consumers every day. As a socially responsible group, we have partnered with various NGOs such as the Tulsi Chanrai Foundation to support their social welfare initiatives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Stock Auditor
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

Job Description


	The ideal candidate will be responsible for inventory verification, stock reconciliation, and ensuring compliance with internal control processes.


Requirements


	Relevant experience in stock auditing or inventory management
	Strong attention to detail and analytical skills
	Ability to work independently and maintain accuracy
	If you&rsquo;re passionate about accountability and operational excellence, we&rsquo;d love to hear from you.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-09",

Stock Auditor Job at AAVA Brands Limited




        
    

        
        AAVA Brands Limited, a new generation company of Chanrai Summit Group (Fareast Mercantile Co. Limited), has been proudly serving Nigeria since 1890 and connects world-class brands to the Nigerian consumer, every day. Now under the umbrella of DP World, AAVA Brands Limited is further strengthened as a flexible and agile service provider. Keeping pace with the evolving needs of African consumers, we strive harder to make the right products available at the right place, in the right time.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We have dedicated our resources to supply chain management through the import / manufacture and distribution of products covering branded Food, Beverages and Fast Moving Consumer Goods for the best multi-nationals in the world. A product portfolio spanning 3,500 SKUs is managed by capable, professional teams, robust infrastructure, and strong delivery systems to ensure availability to consumers every day. As a socially responsible group, we have partnered with various NGOs such as the Tulsi Chanrai Foundation to support their social welfare initiatives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Stock Auditor
Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

Job Description


	The ideal candidate will be responsible for inventory verification, stock reconciliation, and ensuring compliance with internal control processes.


Requirements


	Relevant experience in stock auditing or inventory management
	Strong attention to detail and analytical skills
	Ability to work independently and maintain accuracy
	If you’re passionate about accountability and operational excellence, we’d love to hear from you.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet MIS Executive",
  "description": "AAVA Brands Limited, a new generation company of Chanrai Summit Group (Fareast Mercantile Co. Limited), has been proudly serving Nigeria since 1890 and connects world-class brands to the Nigerian consumer, every day. Now under the umbrella of DP World, AAVA Brands Limited is further strengthened as a flexible and agile service provider. Keeping pace with the evolving needs of African consumers, we strive harder to make the right products available at the right place, in the right time.

We have dedicated our resources to supply chain management through the import / manufacture and distribution of products covering branded Food, Beverages and Fast Moving Consumer Goods for the best multi-nationals in the world. A product portfolio spanning 3,500 SKUs is managed by capable, professional teams, robust infrastructure, and strong delivery systems to ensure availability to consumers every day. As a socially responsible group, we have partnered with various NGOs such as the Tulsi Chanrai Foundation to support their social welfare initiatives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Fleet MIS Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

???????Key Responsibilities


	Maintain and manage fleet-related data using Excel and internal systems
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly MIS reports
	Analyze fleet performance, utilization, and operational metrics
	Create dashboards and reports using Pivot Tables and other Excel tools
	Use functions such as VLOOKUP to manage and organize large datasets
	Ensure data accuracy and consistency across all reports
	Coordinate with Account department for processing of Fleet expenses
	Present reports and insights to management in a clear and structured format
	Identify trends, issues, and improvement opportunities through data analysis
	Required Skills:
	Strong knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, data handling)
	Good data analysis and reporting skills
	Proficiency in preparing presentations (PowerPoint or similar tools)
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Attention to detail and problem-solving ability


Qualifications


	HND in any discipline (preferably in Business, Commerce, or related field)
	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in MIS or data handling roles
	Experience in the automobile or fleet management industry is preferred


Preferred Attributes:


	Ability to work with large datasets efficiently
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Ability to work under deadlines and pressure
	Team player with a proactive approach

Fleet MIS Executive Job at AAVA Brands Limited




        
    

        
        AAVA Brands Limited, a new generation company of Chanrai Summit Group (Fareast Mercantile Co. Limited), has been proudly serving Nigeria since 1890 and connects world-class brands to the Nigerian consumer, every day. Now under the umbrella of DP World, AAVA Brands Limited is further strengthened as a flexible and agile service provider. Keeping pace with the evolving needs of African consumers, we strive harder to make the right products available at the right place, in the right time.

We have dedicated our resources to supply chain management through the import / manufacture and distribution of products covering branded Food, Beverages and Fast Moving Consumer Goods for the best multi-nationals in the world. A product portfolio spanning 3,500 SKUs is managed by capable, professional teams, robust infrastructure, and strong delivery systems to ensure availability to consumers every day. As a socially responsible group, we have partnered with various NGOs such as the Tulsi Chanrai Foundation to support their social welfare initiatives.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet MIS Executive
Job Location: Lagos
Job Type: Full Time

???????Key Responsibilities


	Maintain and manage fleet-related data using Excel and internal systems
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly MIS reports
	Analyze fleet performance, utilization, and operational metrics
	Create dashboards and reports using Pivot Tables and other Excel tools
	Use functions such as VLOOKUP to manage and organize large datasets
	Ensure data accuracy and consistency across all reports
	Coordinate with Account department for processing of Fleet expenses
	Present reports and insights to management in a clear and structured format
	Identify trends, issues, and improvement opportunities through data analysis
	Required Skills:
	Strong knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, data handling)
	Good data analysis and reporting skills
	Proficiency in preparing presentations (PowerPoint or similar tools)
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Attention to detail and problem-solving ability


Qualifications


	HND in any discipline (preferably in Business, Commerce, or related field)
	Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in MIS or data handling roles
	Experience in the automobile or fleet management industry is preferred


Preferred Attributes:


	Ability to work with large datasets efficiently
	Strong organizational and time management skills
	Ability to work under deadlines and pressure
	Team player with a proactive approach
